About Me

Hello, I'm Casey Rogers

With over a decade of experience, I specialize in creating engaging, standard-aligned learning experiences for adult and professional learners, particularly in technical and workforce-focused domains.

Grounded in education and instructional technology, I bring a learner-centered, hands-on approach to design that emphasizes real-world application, problem-solving, and skill transfer.

My experience developing high-impact training for complex technical subjects — mainly Uncrewed Aircraft Systems (UAS) — has strengthened my ability to translate highly sophisticated concepts into clear, accessible, and engaging instruction.

An early foundation in K–12 education gives me a deep understanding of how learners build knowledge across age groups and contexts.

Current Role
Instructional Designer at Mississippi State University (CITE), partnering with faculty to design and review online/hybrid courses using best practices and Quality Matters standards.
My Approach
I apply Instructional Systems Design (ISD) principles to create engaging, accessible, and results-driven learning experiences grounded in theory and informed by data.
Background
Former classroom teacher, curriculum coordinator, and instructional innovator with experience spanning K–12, higher education, and technical workforce training.
What I Bring

Skills & Expertise

My work combines data-informed analysis, creative design, and emerging technologies to transform complex content into clear, learner-centered instruction that improves performance and promotes continuous growth.

Portfolio

Selected Artifacts

These selected examples demonstrate how instructional strategies, visual design, and subject matter expertise are integrated to create meaningful, effective learning experiences that support real-world application and skill acquisition.

UAS / Drone Operations

UAS Training Projects

During my time as Curriculum Coordinator at MSU's ASSURE program, I developed and delivered high-impact training for first responders, law enforcement, and emergency management professionals on Uncrewed Aircraft Systems (UAS). These materials — created in partnership with FEMA, FAA, and DHS — translate complex technical and operational concepts into accessible, scenario-based instruction.

Equipment Overview
Multirotor sUAS
Instructional content covering the advantages and limitations of multirotor small UAS platforms used in first responder operations.
Sensor Technology
RGB & Thermal Sensors
Training materials distinguishing RGB and thermal sensor payloads and their operational applications in search, rescue, and surveillance missions.
Mission Planning
Mission Objectives & Scenario Planning
Scenario-based content helping operators define mission objectives and align flight planning to real-world emergency response situations.
Airspace & Safety
Geofencing & Airspace Restrictions
FEMA-aligned instructional material illustrating geofencing concepts, restricted airspace zones, and safe flight path planning for urban environments.
Data & Intelligence
Data Collection for SAR Missions
Training content addressing terrain awareness, thermal imaging challenges, and color search techniques critical to effective Search and Rescue (SAR) data collection.
Law Enforcement
UAS for Law Enforcement Operations
Instructional materials covering UAS deployment for crime and accident scene documentation, offering birds-eye perspective and faster scene processing than traditional methods.
Rapid Deployment
Quick Deployment & Multi-Agency Support
Training content on rapid UAS deployment for missing persons, fleeing suspects, and multi-agency coordination with fire departments and other LEO entities.
Program Reflection

MSIT Reflection

Motivated by the need to improve training courses for first responders learning drone operations, I enrolled in the MSIT program to develop stronger instructional design skills.

Throughout the program, I gained knowledge of key theories and strategies such as instructional graphics, cognitive load theory, dual-channel processing, and Gagne's Nine Events of Instruction.

The program also helped me build critical skills in needs analysis, aligning instruction with organizational goals, and using technology intentionally to support learning.

Hands-on projects and real-world applications prepared me to confidently address instructional challenges in my career. Overall, the MSIT program provided a strong foundation in both theory and practice.
